channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.description: There are many ways to build apps and systems. In today’s AI-native world, the possibilities are endless. Now imagine you’re tasked with building a multi-agent research system, with one key...

item.title: MLOps Explained: The Backbone of Production Machine Learning.
item.description: The modern digital systems are becoming increasingly powered by Machine Learning models. The development of a model is, however, not all. Lifecycle management of machine learning models in...

item.title: Pare de usar isEmpty() e isBlank() em seus Controllers Spring Boot
item.description: É comum encontrarmos em projetos Spring Boot validações como isEmpty(), != null ou isBlank() aplicadas diretamente nos métodos dos Controllers. Embora pareça uma solução rápida, essa prática é um...

item.title: Your AI Agents Deserve the Same Ops Treatment as Your Microservices
item.description: A few months ago I was looking at how our team was actually running AI agents in production. One was a Python script in a tmux session on someone's laptop. Another was a cron job with no timeout....

item.title: Stop Prompting, Start Forging: A New Era of Professional SDLC
item.description: We’ve all been there: asking a LLM to implement a complex feature, only to get a 200-line solution that fails immediately. The problem isn't the AI's intelligence. It's the Medium. A chat window...

item.title: 7 AI Prompts Senior Engineers Use to Build Production Code Faster
item.description: AI coding tools are powerful — but most developers still use them like autocomplete. The difference between average AI usage and 10x productivity often comes down to one thing: how you prompt the...

item.title: The MacBook Neo is a winner
item.description: It was a little surprising to see Apple decide to leap fully into the affordable laptop market, to try and compete with devices the company typically prefers to just look down its nose at. It was...

item.title: FREE AI CODE ASSISTANTS THAT ACTUALLY WORK 2

item.title: sql and databases
item.description: What is a database. A database is an organized collection of data stored electronically. What is SQL SQL stands for structured query language. It is used for storing data, retrieving data,...

item.title: PSA: Don’t buy a $4,400 gray market Samsung TriFold on eBay
item.description: I had to buy Samsung's Galaxy Z TriFold from eBay for $4,399 and I'm pretty sure that's because Samsung doesn't want anyone to actually have this phone. I'm also afraid to turn it on. Not that...

item.title: Zero-Dependency HTTP Fingerprinting Library in Go: Bot Detection with JA3
item.description: Last month I was building an API for a fintech project. We had rate limiting in place (using kazrl, actually), but bots kept slipping through. They'd rotate IPs, spoof User-Agents, and our simple...

item.title: Google's AI Searches Love to Refer You Back to Google
item.description: The company's generative AI search tools increasingly cite its own services, like Google Search and YouTube, over third-party publishers.

item.title: The Download: how AI is used for military targeting, and the Pentagon’s war on Claude
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Defense official reveals how AI chatbots could be used for...

item.title: China’s OpenClaw Boom Is a Gold Rush for AI Companies
item.description: Hype around the open source agent is driving people to rent cloud servers and buy AI subscriptions just to try it, creating a windfall for tech companies.

item.title: PC makers are not ready for the MacBook Neo
item.description: The MacBook Neo is here, and it took no time at all for an executive from a major PC manufacturer to put their foot in their mouth trying to discuss this new competition from Apple's $600 laptop....

item.title: Apple reduces App Store fees in China to ward off regulators
item.description: Apple is cutting App Store developer fees in China from March 15th, dropping its typical commission on purchases from 30 percent down to 25 percent. The decision was made following "discussions...

item.title: Academia and the “AI Brain Drain”
item.description: In 2025, Google, Amazon, Microsoft and Meta collectively spent US$380 billion on building artificial-intelligence tools. That number is expected to surge still higher this year, to $650 billion,...

item.title: Gamers’ Worst Nightmares About AI Are Coming True
item.description: From the global RAM shortage driving up console prices to job loss in the industry, gaming is shaping up to be one of the AI boom's biggest casualties.

item.title: Palantir Demos Show How the Military Could Use AI Chatbots to Generate War Plans
item.description: Software demos and Pentagon records detail how chatbots like Anthropic’s Claude could help the Pentagon analyze intelligence and suggest next steps.

item.title: Google Fixes Two Chrome Zero-Days Exploited in the Wild Affecting Skia and V8
item.description: Google on Thursday released security updates for its Chrome web browser to address two high-severity vulnerabilities that it said have been exploited in the wild. The list of vulnerabilities is as...

item.title: Future AI chips could be built on glass
item.description: Human-made glass is thousands of years old. But it’s now poised to find its way into the AI chips used in the world’s newest and largest data centers. This year, a South Korean company called...

item.title: Nine CrackArmor Flaws in Linux AppArmor Enable Root Escalation, Bypass Container Isolation
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed multiple security vulnerabilities within the Linux kernel's AppArmor module that could be exploited by unprivileged users to circumvent kernel protections,...

item.title: Authorities Disrupt SocksEscort Proxy Botnet Exploiting 369,000 IPs Across 163 Countries
item.description: A court-authorized international law enforcement operation has dismantled a criminal proxy service named SocksEscort that enslaved thousands of residential routers worldwide into a botnet for...

item.title: Before quantum computing arrives, this startup wants enterprises already running on it
item.description: After selling his AI startup to AMD for $665 million, Peter Sarlin is back with QuTwo, a new venture building the infrastructure it believes enterprises will need when quantum computing finally...

item.title: Truecaller now lets you hang up on scammers — on behalf of your family
item.description: Caller identity platform Truecaller recently launched a new feature that lets one person become an admin of a family group, get alerts about fraud calls received by other members, and even end a...

item.title: Veeam Patches 7 Critical Backup & Replication Flaws Allowing Remote Code Execution
item.description: Veeam has released security updates to address multiple critical vulnerabilities in its Backup & Replication software that, if successfully exploited, could result in remote code execution. The...

item.title: Build an Agent That Thinks Like a Data Scientist: How We Hit #1 on DABStep with Reusable Tool Generation
How to watch Jensen Huang’s Nvidia GTC 2026 keynote
